title: fix: handle ISO string and numeric timestamps in TimeWeightedVectorSt…

labels.name: fix
labels.description: For PRs that implement a fix

body:
…oreRetriever



Fix TypeError when documents have timestamp metadata stored as ISO strings or numeric timestamps instead of datetime objects. The retriever now properly converts these common formats to datetime before time decay calculations.


I was testing TimeWeightedVectorStoreRetriever with documents loaded from a JSON file where timestamps were stored as ISO strings (e.g., "2024-01-01T12:00:00"). When I called retriever.invoke("query"), it crashed with TypeError: unsupported operand type(s) for -: 'datetime.datetime' and 'str' because the retriever tried to subtract a string from a datetime when calculating time decay. The fix now properly handles ISO strings, numeric timestamps, and datetime objects in metadata.


# PR Description

## Summary
Fix `TypeError` in `TimeWeightedVectorStoreRetriever` when documents have timestamp metadata stored as ISO strings or numeric timestamps instead of datetime objects. The retriever now properly converts these common formats to datetime before time decay calculations.

## Bug Discovery
I was testing `TimeWeightedVectorStoreRetriever` with documents loaded from a JSON file where timestamps were stored as ISO strings (e.g., `"2024-01-01T12:00:00"`). When I called `retriever.invoke("query")`, it crashed with `TypeError: unsupported operand type(s) for -: 'datetime.datetime' and 'str'` because the retriever tried to subtract a string from a datetime when calculating time decay.

## Changes
- Enhanced `_document_get_date()` method to handle ISO 8601 strings, numeric timestamps (int/float), and datetime objects
- Added fallback to `datetime.now()` for unrecognized formats to prevent crashes
- Added unit tests for ISO string and numeric timestamp handling

## Testing
- Added unit tests in `test_time_weighted_retriever_dates.py` that verify:
  - ISO 8601 string timestamps are properly converted
  - Numeric timestamp values are properly converted
  - Existing datetime object handling remains unchanged

## AI Assistance Disclaimer
This PR was created with assistance from AI tools for code analysis, bug identification, and implementation. The fix addresses a real type safety issue in the retriever's date handling logic.
